text.title: OpenAI Function Calling — Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules for tool-use and structured-extraction calls. Source: OpenAI Function Calling guide, Structured Outputs docs, production failure logs (2026).

rule.statement: Append the assistant message (with `tool_calls`) to `messages` BEFORE appending any `role: tool` results. Sort tool results by matching `tool_call_id` order from the assistant message.
rule.rationale: The API rejects tool results that arrive without a preceding assistant message with `tool_calls`; out-of-order results corrupt the model's next reasoning step.

rule.statement: Use `client.beta.chat.completions.parse` only on `gpt-4o` and newer. For older models, fall back to `json_object` mode + manual Pydantic validation.
rule.rationale: parse is not supported pre-gpt-4o; calling it raises NotImplementedError. Capability gating prevents silent fallback to wrong behaviour.

rule.statement: Before reading `response.choices[0].message.parsed`, check `message.refusal`. If non-null, raise with the refusal text. Never AttributeError on a None `parsed`.
rule.rationale: Safety refusals return `refusal` populated and `parsed=None`; bare attribute access crashes the pipeline.

rule.statement: Before executing parallel tool calls that write external state, run a coherence check: no contradictory pairs (create+delete on same id, debit+credit same account at the same step).
rule.rationale: Default `parallel_tool_calls=true` lets the model batch contradictory writes; corruption races the second tool's return.

rule.statement: Set `tool_choice="required"` when the pipeline depends on a tool result. Set `tool_choice={"type":"function","function":{"name":""}}` to force a specific tool. Default `auto` only for exploratory calls.
rule.rationale: `auto` lets the model answer from context, skipping the tool entirely; pipelines that expect a tool result then fail downstream.

rule.statement: Tool callables are registered in a single `tool_registry: dict[name, callable]` before the call. The dispatch loop looks up by `function.name`, never `eval` or dynamic import.
rule.rationale: Dynamic import / eval on model-supplied names is an arbitrary-code-execution sink; the registry is the only authoritative dispatch.
